Subset a MiltSeries to a time window

Source: R/series-manipulate.R
milt_window.Rd

Returns only observations within [start, end] (both inclusive). Either bound may be NULL to impose no limit on that side.

Usage

milt_window(series, start = NULL, end = NULL, group = NULL)

Arguments

series

A MiltSeries object.

start

Start of the window (Date, POSIXct, or NULL).

end

End of the window (Date, POSIXct, or NULL).

group

Optional group value for grouped series. When supplied, only observations from that group are retained before applying the time window.

Value

A MiltSeries containing only the windowed observations.

See also

milt_split(), milt_split_at()

Other series: milt_add_covariates(), milt_concat(), milt_diagnose(), milt_fill_gaps(), milt_get_covariates(), milt_head(), milt_plot_acf(), milt_plot_decomp(), milt_resample(), milt_series(), milt_split(), milt_split_at(), milt_tail(), plot.MiltSeries()

Examples

s <- milt_series(AirPassengers)
milt_window(s, start = as.Date("1953-01-01"), end = as.Date("1956-12-01"))
#> # A MiltSeries: 48 x 1 [monthly]
#> # Time range : 1953 Jan — 1956 Dec
#> # Components : value
#> # Gaps       : none
#> # A tibble: 6 × 2
#>   time       value
#>   <date>     <dbl>
#> 1 1953-01-01   196
#> 2 1953-02-01   196
#> 3 1953-03-01   236
#> 4 1953-04-01   235
#> 5 1953-05-01   229
#> 6 1953-06-01   243
#> # … with 42 more rows
